message:
The other day, I was in the kitchen, and my 8-year-old was trying to help me make dinner. As we chopped veggies, I suddenly remembered the importance of kitchen safety. I turned to him and said, "You know, buddy, we should always keep a pot of water handy in case something catches fire."

He raised an eyebrow and asked, "But what if it catches fire? Isn’t that a little scary?" I chuckled, realizing how seriously he took my words. I explained that it’s not about being scared, but about being prepared. We talked about our escape plan and how to stay safe in the house. 

I loved seeing his eyes light up as he took on the role of the safety captain. It reminded me that even the simplest conversations can empower our kids to think ahead.

So, do you have a little safety talk with your kids? What do you find they’re most curious about? This little refresher helped us a lot!
